body.question_1: How would you rate the technical competency of the student intern in their relevant field of study?
body.answer_1: The intern from the School of Communication's Public Relations program demonstrated commendable technical competency that significantly exceeded our initial expectations for a student at this stage of their academic career. Their foundational knowledge in core discipline areas was solid, well-structured, and readily applicable to real workplace challenges encountered during the internship period. They adapted quickly to our internal tools, methodologies, and technical standards, requiring minimal remedial instruction before becoming a productive and largely self-directed contributor to assigned projects. The depth of practical skills, particularly in applied problem-solving and technical documentation, was notably strong and reflects well on the quality of the School of Communication's curriculum design and delivery.

body.question_2: How well did the intern demonstrate professional communication and interpersonal skills in the workplace?
body.answer_2: This intern's professionalism was acceptable, showing genuine commitment and a positive attitude that formed a solid foundation, with some areas requiring coaching and development during the placement period. Reliability and punctuality were good overall, though proactive communication about progress and obstacles was inconsistent and required encouragement from supervisors. The intern engaged positively with team members and accepted feedback constructively in most situations, demonstrating the willingness to learn that is essential for professional growth. Additional preparation in formal workplace communication — particularly written reporting standards and stakeholder update practices — would improve the professional impact of graduates from this program.

body.question_3: How effectively did the intern collaborate with team members and contribute to group projects?
body.answer_3: The intern's ability to collaborate effectively within a team environment was concerning and required more management attention than anticipated. Contribution to group tasks was often uneven — the intern sometimes worked in isolation rather than coordinating with colleagues, leading to duplicated effort and avoidable misalignments in shared workstreams. Communication within the team was reactive rather than proactive, and the intern occasionally struggled with the shared accountability and compromise that effective team collaboration demands. We recommend the School of Communication incorporate more structured team-based project work and collaborative simulation into the Public Relations curriculum to better prepare students for the realities of modern workplace team dynamics.

body.question_4: How would you assess the intern's problem-solving abilities and approach to challenging or unfamiliar tasks?
body.answer_4: The intern's problem-solving abilities were among the most superb attributes they brought to our organization from the School of Communication's Public Relations program. When confronted with unfamiliar challenges or technical obstacles, the intern approached them systematically — breaking problems into manageable components, forming and testing hypotheses, and escalating appropriately only when genuinely blocked by limitations beyond their current capability. Their intellectual curiosity, persistence under pressure, and willingness to explore unconventional solutions made them a genuinely valuable problem-solver within the team. This quality is difficult to teach in the workplace and speaks highly of the critical thinking culture embedded in the School of Communication's academic programs.

body.question_5: How quickly and effectively did the intern adapt to your company's work culture, tools, and environment?
body.answer_5: Adaptability to changing conditions and evolving requirements was a concerning challenge for this intern from the School of Communication's Public Relations program, and it became a recurring obstacle during the internship period. When project scope or priorities shifted — as they regularly do in a professional environment — the intern's response was often slow and required significant reassurance and directive guidance from supervisors. Tolerance for ambiguity and the ability to operate effectively without complete information or a fully defined task specification were noticeably underdeveloped relative to our expectations. We recommend the School of Communication expose students to more dynamic, ambiguity-tolerant project environments during their studies to develop this critical professional competency before graduation.

body.question_6: How proficient was the intern in English communication, both written and verbal, for professional purposes?
body.answer_6: The intern's English communication proficiency — both written and spoken — was commendable and met the professional standards required for effective participation in our bilingual team environment. Written reports, emails, and documentation were consistently clear, professionally structured, and free of errors that would compromise understanding or credibility in a client-facing context. Verbal communication in meetings and collaborative sessions was confident and coherent, enabling full participation in discussions with both local and international colleagues. The intern's English proficiency reflects well on the language preparation embedded in the School of Communication's Public Relations program and is a strong asset in our increasingly international work environment.

body.question_7: How well did the intern's academic training align with the practical demands and standards of your industry?
body.answer_7: Based on our experience with this intern from the School of Communication's Public Relations program, we are remarkablely impressed with the alignment between the academic curriculum and the practical demands of our industry. The foundational knowledge, theoretical frameworks, and applied skills the intern brought to the role mapped closely to what we require in graduate-level professional practitioners in this field. In particular, the project-based and problem-solving components of the curriculum appear to have translated well into workplace readiness. We would encourage the School of Communication to continue developing its industry connections and to involve practitioners actively in curriculum review to maintain this strong alignment as the professional landscape continues to evolve.

body.question_8: What specific technical or soft skill areas do you believe the university curriculum should strengthen for future graduates?
body.answer_8: Our curriculum-related feedback for the School of Communication's Public Relations program, based on this internship experience, is fair: there is a reasonable foundation but meaningful gaps that the institution could address to significantly improve graduate readiness. Core theoretical knowledge is adequately covered, and the intern was able to apply foundational concepts with reasonable effectiveness. However, gaps exist in applied tool proficiency, project management fundamentals, and the professional communication standards expected in industry settings. We recommend more regular and structured industry consultations to ensure the curriculum remains current, and greater emphasis on workplace simulation through live projects, internships, and industry-sponsored assessments.

body.question_9: How would you rate the intern's overall performance and value contribution to your organization during the placement?
body.answer_9: The overall performance of this intern from the School of Communication's Public Relations program has been impressive and has added genuine value to our organization during the internship period in ways we did not fully anticipate at the outset. Key deliverables were completed on time, to the required quality standard, and in some cases with additional initiative that exceeded the brief. The intern's contributions to ongoing team projects have been meaningful and will have a lasting impact beyond the duration of their placement. We are very satisfied with the investment we made in hosting this intern and have recommended internally that we continue and expand our partnership with the School of Communication's internship program in the coming academic year.

body.question_10: Based on your experience with this intern, would you consider hiring from this university in the future, and why?
body.answer_10: We would cautiously consider hiring from the School of Communication's Public Relations program again, with the expectation that the skill and readiness gaps we observed in this internship experience would be addressed through curriculum improvements in the near term. The intern's core attitude and growth potential were positive, and these qualities form a solid basis for future professional development. However, we would want to see stronger applied technical skills, better English communication preparation, and more structured professional readiness training before we significantly expand our intake from this institution. We encourage the School of Communication to reach out to us directly to establish a closer industry-academia collaboration that benefits both future interns and our organization's talent development strategy.
